corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: فَأَنسَىٰهُ needs whole-token proof for فَ plus finite causative/weak-root verb host and هُ object before a hover such as 'so he made him forget' can be certified
drill_prompt: Segment فَأَنسَىٰهُ into proclitic, verb host, and object suffix; explain why a root infinitive is unsafe.
learner_explanation: فَأَنسَىٰهُ is not simply 'to forget'. It contains فَ, a finite verb meaning someone caused forgetting, and هُ meaning him/it.
nahw_lesson: The fā' relation and pronoun referent affect the contextual wording, so the row stays gated until the clause is reviewed.
sarf_lesson: Weak or hamzated finite verbs require exact form, voice, subject, and object suffix review; component evidence cannot certify the whole token.
what_failed: Component evidence from the ن س ي entry produced a dictionary infinitive on a finite written token with visible proclitic and suffix.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: ذَرْهُمْ needs imperative/weak-root review plus هُمْ object before an authored contribution such as 'leave them' can be certified
drill_prompt: Explain why ذَرْهُمْ should not be glossed as 'to leave someone or something'.
learner_explanation: ذَرْهُمْ includes the command 'leave' and the attached pronoun هُمْ, 'them'. The hover must show both pieces.
nahw_lesson: Command force and the object referent are contextual; a bare root gloss cannot stand in for the clause role.
sarf_lesson: Imperatives and weak-root forms are finite token contributions, not dictionary headwords; suffix objects must remain visible.
what_failed: A finite imperative with an object suffix inherited the entry infinitive.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: ٱلذِّكْرَ is a definite noun in this slot; do not use verb-family infinitive prose without exact-address noun/sense review
drill_prompt: Contrast ٱلذِّكْرَ with ذُكِرَ and ذَكَرٍ: name the POS/voice evidence before choosing a hover.
learner_explanation: ٱلذِّكْرَ has the article and noun shape. A learner should not see a 'to remember' verb definition for this token.
nahw_lesson: The nominal role and object/case context decide whether 'the reminder/remembrance' is appropriate; it is not a finite verb contribution.
sarf_lesson: ذَكَر, ذِكْر, and ذُكِرَ are different POS/voice shapes; vowels and article/case are certification evidence, not polish.
visible_bad_hover: to take note of, mention; remember; remind vigorously
what_failed: A definite nominal surface in the ذ ك ر family received a verb infinitive hover.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: ذُكِرَ is a passive finite verb surface and cannot be certified through the noun entry without voice/POS review
drill_prompt: Identify the passive vowel pattern in ذُكِرَ and explain why it cannot share the noun hover for ذَكَرٍ.
learner_explanation: ذُكِرَ means a mentioning occurred to the token; it is not the noun 'male' or a dictionary 'to remember' entry.
nahw_lesson: The passive clause decides the agentless wording, but the hover must first preserve passive voice.
sarf_lesson: Damma-kasra passive signatures and finite verb morphology override root-family or noun-entry spread.
visible_bad_hover: to take note of, mention; remember; remind vigorously
what_failed: A passive verb-looking token was linked under a noun/source family and shown with broad infinitive prose.
